methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

private.name: Stark Defence
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

private.name: Vatn Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $300M

private.name: Ares Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

data.narrative:
Valinor Enterprises is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company founded by Trae Stephens and Grant Verstandig, having raised $85M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 10 comparable companies: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries operating in the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression modeling treats the $85M in total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implying a $170M valuation. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables—Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Ares Industries at $150M—yielding a median valuation of $300M with a range of $150M to $500M.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated valuation range of $144M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $235M. The peer-median method places Valinor in the mid-to-upper tier of its peer cohort, while stage-progression implies more conservative positioning. Key uncertainties include the timing and terms of the next financing round, relative competitive positioning against the five-company peer subset, and the extent to which public-market comparables (trading at lower multiples) should influence this private valuation.
